title: How Python's GIL Works
description: An interactive visual guide explaining Python's Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) — what it is, why it exists, and how it affects threading and multiprocessing.
codeBlock:
import threading

def task(n):
    total = 0
    for i in range(n):
        total += i
    return total

# Two threads, but only one runs at a time
# due to the GIL
details:
The Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) is a mutex that protects access to Python objects, preventing multiple threads from executing Python bytecode simultaneously in a single interpreter. This interactive visual guide explores how the GIL works and its implications for concurrent programming in Python.

Launch the interactive visual guide →
